frameworks.name: free energy principle
frameworks.one_line: proposed unifying account of brain function via free energy minimization

frameworks.name: Bayesian inference
frameworks.one_line: related unifying account closely linked to and partly subsumed by FEP

frameworks.name: predictive coding
frameworks.one_line: earlier unifying idea connected to and partly subsumed by FEP

frameworks.name: active learning
frameworks.one_line: earlier unifying idea connected to and partly subsumed by FEP

core_question: What does the free energy principle actually predict about the brain that overlapping frameworks do not?
key_assumptions: the brain can be characterized by a single unifying principle
Bayesian inference, predictive coding, and active learning share formal structure with FEP
key_findings: FEP overlaps with and in some cases subsumes Bayesian inference, predictive coding, and active learning
the frameworks share some predictions but also have distinctive predictions
interpretations: clarifying the relationships separates FEP-specific commitments from those shared with related accounts
